summary: The test suite covers the four core library functions (parseDependencies, checkLicenseCompliance, parseRequirementsTxt, generateComplianceReport) with clean, readable tests and a solid mock strategy. However, significant gaps exist. The 'unknown' status is only tested when the lookup returns undefined; the case where a license exists but appears in neither list is never exercised. The CLI (cli.ts) has zero test coverage despite handling file I/O and error paths that were explicitly required. Error-handling scenarios (missing file, malformed JSON, unsupported manifest type) are untested. The required workflow structure tests (YAML triggers, job structure, script path existence, actionlint exit-code assertion) are entirely absent — a stated requirement. devDependencies vs dependencies distinction is not tested, nor is mixed-status reporting (approved+denied+unknown in one report, checking all three counters simultaneously). The Map-based mock is clean and the describe-block organization is good, but the suite would miss regressions in the error-handling and CLI layers and cannot validate the CI/CD workflow requirements.
